column2: #   Estimate whether the first stage differs across covariate subgroups,
#   after controlling for the Card covariates.
#   D_i = alpha + rho Z_i + X_i beta + theta (Z_i * G_i) + gamma G_i + u_i
# For region heterogeneity, use region as a factor:
#   D_i = alpha + region_i + Z_i:region_i + other controls + u_i
# Then each region-specific first stage is the coefficient on Z within that
# region, controlling for the remaining covariates.
# 1. Construct a region factor from reg661-reg668
# Main Z coefficient = first stage for G=0
# Interaction coefficient = difference in first stage for G=1 vs G=0
int_name <- if (int_name_1 %in% names(co)) int_name_1 else int_name_2
# 3. Function for region-specific adjusted first stages
# Using 0 + region66 creates one intercept per region.
# Z_nearc4:region66 gives one first-stage coefficient per region.
cn <- if (coef_name %in% names(co)) coef_name else coef_name_alt
N_Z1 = sum(data$region66 == r & data$Z_nearc4 == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
N_Z0 = sum(data$region66 == r & data$Z_nearc4 == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
# For binary subgroup interactions, include standard Card controls.
# Important: when G is one of the controls, remove it from the controls list
# For region-specific heterogeneity, do NOT include individual region dummies
# 7. Add ratios relative to the overall adjusted first stage
D_somecol ~ Z_nearc4 + exper + expersq + black + south + smsa + smsa66 +
reg661 + reg662 + reg663 + reg664 + reg665 + reg666 + reg667 + reg668,
D_educ16 ~ Z_nearc4 + exper + expersq + black + south + smsa + smsa66 +
reg661 + reg662 + reg663 + reg664 + reg665 + reg666 + reg667 + reg668,
cat("Region results with ratios — D1 = somecol:\n")
Region results with ratios — D1 = somecol:
cat("Region results with ratios — D2 = educ16:\n")
Region results with ratios — D2 = educ16:
cat("  Adj_first_stage is the covariate-adjusted effect of nearc4 on D within the subgroup/region.\n")
Adj_first_stage is the covariate-adjusted effect of nearc4 on D within the subgroup/region.
cat("  For binary groups, it comes from D ~ Z * G + controls.\n")
For binary groups, it comes from D ~ Z * G + controls.
cat("  For regions, it comes from D ~ region + Z:region + controls.\n")
For regions, it comes from D ~ region + Z:region + controls.
cat("  Large positive values mean the instrument creates many compliers in that group.\n")
Large positive values mean the instrument creates many compliers in that group.
cat("  Near-zero values mean weak/no first stage in that group.\n")
Near-zero values mean weak/no first stage in that group.
cat("  Negative values mean the raw/adjusted relationship reverses in that group; treat cautiously,\n")
Negative values mean the raw/adjusted relationship reverses in that group; treat cautiously,
cat("  especially when N_Z0 or N_Z1 is small.\n")
especially when N_Z0 or N_Z1 is small.
cat("  Ratio_to_overall_adj compares the region-specific first stage to the overall adjusted first stage.\n")
Ratio_to_overall_adj compares the region-specific first stage to the overall adjusted first stage.
cat("  Values above 1 mean the region contributes more strongly to the complier margin than average.\n")
Values above 1 mean the region contributes more strongly to the complier margin than average.
cat("  Values below 1 mean it contributes less strongly than average.\n")
Values below 1 mean it contributes less strongly than average.
